preamble: Palatalization of the voiceless dental fricative [θ] to the voiceless post-alveolar fricative [ʃ] (represented here as 'sh') occurs in specific phonetic environments, particularly before high vowels or as part of expressive phonological systems.

examples.note: Regular sound change where the interdental fricative *θ merged with sibilants and shifted to the postalveolar position [ʃ]. Arabic preserved the original [θ] (thalāth).
Used in 'expressive palatalization' to convey smallness or emotional value (e.g., in diminutives).
The reconstructed phoneme *θ shifted to [ʃ] before high front vowels (*i, *iː) and palatal glides.

sources: Huehnergard, J. (2019). Proto-Semitic Language and Culture.
